tossups.question: A work by this artist depicts a giant gun pointing at a banker with a head based on a photograph of the chemist John Herschel. Near the center of a work by this artist, a headless dancer appears to throw a woman’s head up towards an elephant. This artist depicted an African statue with a flapper’s legs in The Sweet One, an entry in the Ethnographic Museum series. This artist of High Finance was the lover and sometimes collaborator of the artist who created Mechanical Head (The Spirit of Our Time), Raoul Hausmann. The top part of a piece by this artist features men like Friedrich Ebert, while people like (*) Kathe Kollwitz, John Heartfield, Marx, and Lenin appear in its bottom half. That artwork of this artist uses newspaper cuttings to create phrases like “Invest your money in Dada!” For 10 points, name this artist of Cut with the Kitchen Knife Dada through the Last Weimar Beer Belly Cultural Epoch of Germany, a female pioneer of photomontage.
tossups.question_sanitized: A work by this artist depicts a giant gun pointing at a banker with a head based on a photograph of the chemist John Herschel. Near the center of a work by this artist, a headless dancer appears to throw a woman's head up towards an elephant. This artist depicted an African statue with a flapper's legs in The Sweet One, an entry in the Ethnographic Museum series. This artist of High Finance was the lover and sometimes collaborator of the artist who created Mechanical Head (The Spirit of Our Time), Raoul Hausmann. The top part of a piece by this artist features men like Friedrich Ebert, while people like (*) Kathe Kollwitz, John Heartfield, Marx, and Lenin appear in its bottom half. That artwork of this artist uses newspaper cuttings to create phrases like "Invest your money in Dada!" For 10 points, name this artist of Cut with the Kitchen Knife Dada through the Last Weimar Beer Belly Cultural Epoch of Germany, a female pioneer of photomontage.

bonuses.leadin: This artist changed his name from a more “German” Helmut Herzfeld. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist, known for works such as The Hand has 5 Fingers. He is said to be one of the pioneers of using art as a political weapon.
Specific term required. Heartfield was known for works in this medium, in which photos are cut, pasted, and rearranged to create a new, composite image.
This Dada artist was known for photomontages such as Kleine Sonne and Cut with the Kitchen Knife.
answers: Hannah Höch This artist’s anti-Nazi beliefs led her to contribute to Retrato de la Burguesia.
answers_sanitized: Hannah Hoch This artist's anti-Nazi beliefs led her to contribute to Retrato de la Burguesia.

parts_sanitized: Name this artist, known for works such as The Hand has 5 Fingers. He is said to be one of the pioneers of using art as a political weapon.
Specific term required. Heartfield was known for works in this medium, in which photos are cut, pasted, and rearranged to create a new, composite image.

bonuses.leadin: An artist with this surname created an assemblage that features a plumb bob, watch gear, strings, and a watercolor background, called A Menaced Male and Female. For 10 points:
parts: Give this surname shared by Suzanne and her brother, who created works such as Fountain and Nude Descending a Staircase, No. 2.

A letter from Marcel to Suzanne may suggest that Elsa von Freytag-Loringhoven created Duchamp’s Fountain, the most famous example of this specific type of work. Give either the English or French term.
This artist created montages of magazine cutouts and photos of artifacts for the series From an Ethnographic Museum. She also created the quasi-automotive photomontage The Beautiful Girl.
answers: Duchamp [accept Marcel Duchamp or Henri-Robert-Marcel Duchamp; accept Suzanne Duchamp or Suzanne Duchamp-Crotti]
answers_sanitized: Duchamp [accept Marcel Duchamp or Henri-Robert-Marcel Duchamp; accept Suzanne Duchamp or Suzanne Duchamp-Crotti]
readymade [accept found objects or objets trouves]
Hannah Hoch [or Anna Therese Johanne Hoch]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: An artist with this surname created an assemblage that features a plumb bob, watch gear, strings, and a watercolor background, called A Menaced Male and Female. For 10 points:

This artist created montages of magazine cutouts and photos of artifacts for the series From an Ethnographic Museum. She also created the quasi-automotive photomontage The Beautiful Girl.

bonuses.leadin: The development of representational art is analyzed from a psychological perspective in an E. H. Gombrich essay titled for Meditations on [one of these objects]. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these objects. The name for one of these objects is referenced in an unrelated anecdote involving the German writer Richard Huelsenbeck and a letter opener.
That anecdote is one of the possible origins of the name of this avant-garde artistic movement, which was described by Tristan Tzara as a “virgin microbe.” Its other members included Marcel Duchamp.
This artist included newspaper clippings of the word “Dada” among cutouts of wheels and disembodied heads in the massive photomontage Cut With the Kitchen Knife.

bonuses.leadin: A work by this artist depicts a woman with a lightbulb for a head and has a series of BMW logos in the background. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist of The Beautiful Girl. This Dada artist pioneered using photomontage collages.
Hannah Höch’s most famous photomontage is titled for and depicts various people from this country’s Weimar Republic.

Due to the political nature of her work, the Nazi party branded Höch’s work by this term. An art exhibition with this title organized by the Nazis, which displayed paintings confiscated from museums, coincided with the Great German Art Exhibition, which exhibited approved art.

packet.name: E. McGill A, NYU A, Purdue A

answers_sanitized: Hannah Hoch [or Anna Therese Johanne Hoch]
Germany [or Deutschland; or Federal Republic of Germany]
degenerate art [or Degenerate Art Exhibition; or Entartete Kunst]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A work by this artist depicts a woman with a lightbulb for a head and has a series of BMW logos in the background. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this artist of The Beautiful Girl. This Dada artist pioneered using photomontage collages.
Hannah Hoch's most famous photomontage is titled for and depicts various people from this country's Weimar Republic.

Due to the political nature of her work, the Nazi party branded Hoch's work by this term. An art exhibition with this title organized by the Nazis, which displayed paintings confiscated from museums, coincided with the Great German Art Exhibition, which exhibited approved art.

bonuses.leadin: This artist developed screen projections for a production of The Mother by Bertolt Brecht (“breck’t”), who later helped him get admitted to the East Germany Academy of the Arts. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who also illustrated several covers for Upton Sinclair. One of this artist’s best known images depicts a certain figure’s torso as transparent, with his ribs visible above a pile of coins.

This world leader was the frequent target of Heartfield’s satirical photomontages. In a photo overlaid with flowers, this man is shown posing with a young Jewish girl who was known as “the Führer’s child.”

This artist overlaid part of a still of Renée Falconetti with a Cameroonian mask in her series From an Ethnographic Museum. Heartfield appears in a photomontage by her featuring several cutouts of the word “dada.”
answers: Hannah Höch (“hoke” or “hosh” or “hokh” or “herk” or “hersh” or “herkh”) [or Anna Therese Johanne Höch]
answers_sanitized: Hannah Hoch ("hoke" or "hosh" or "hokh" or "herk" or "hersh" or "herkh") [or Anna Therese Johanne Hoch]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This artist developed screen projections for a production of The Mother by Bertolt Brecht ("breck't"), who later helped him get admitted to the East Germany Academy of the Arts. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this artist who also illustrated several covers for Upton Sinclair. One of this artist's best known images depicts a certain figure's torso as transparent, with his ribs visible above a pile of coins.

This world leader was the frequent target of Heartfield's satirical photomontages. In a photo overlaid with flowers, this man is shown posing with a young Jewish girl who was known as "the Fuhrer's child."

This artist overlaid part of a still of Renee Falconetti with a Cameroonian mask in her series From an Ethnographic Museum. Heartfield appears in a photomontage by her featuring several cutouts of the word "dada."

bonuses.leadin: One member of this art movement paired poems and collages in her Picture Book. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this avant-garde group of artists that included Man Ray and Marcel Duchamp. It takes its name from a word meaning “hobby-horse.”
Name this Dada artist and photomontage pioneer who criticized the condition of women in her “Cut with the Dada Kitchen Knife through the Last Weimar Beer-Belly Cultural Epoch in Germany.”
Otto Dix was influenced by Dada to include collage in his work. Dix was a member of this art movement, whose representative works include The Eclipse of the Sun and the Departure triptych.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: One member of this art movement paired poems and collages in her Picture Book. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this avant-garde group of artists that included Man Ray and Marcel Duchamp. It takes its name from a word meaning "hobby-horse."
Name this Dada artist and photomontage pioneer who criticized the condition of women in her "Cut with the Dada Kitchen Knife through the Last Weimar Beer-Belly Cultural Epoch in Germany."
Otto Dix was influenced by Dada to include collage in his work. Dix was a member of this art movement, whose representative works include The Eclipse of the Sun and the Departure triptych.

bonuses.leadin: This artist slapped Paul von Hindenburg’s face on top of an exotic dancer’s body in the top-right corner of a collage that sets von Hindenburg among the anti-modernists. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this German artist of the Berlin Dadaists. She is known for her photomontages, such as Cut with the Kitchen-Knife Dada through the Last Weimar Beer-Belly Cultural Epoch of Germany.

George Grosz, a sometime Dadaist whose head was included in Hoch’s collage, painted this demented vision of a “dance of death” that shows a skeleton chugging alcohol on top of a coffin amidst a throng of citydwellers.

This Dadaist created a series of abstract paintings he called “aerographs” as well as other paintings like A Night at Saint-Jean de Luz. He was also a photographer who took pictures of Marcel Duchamp in drag.
answers: Dedication to Oskar Panizza [or The Funeral: Dedication to Oskar Panizza; or The Funeral Procession; or To Oskar Panizza]
answers_sanitized: Dedication to Oskar Panizza [or The Funeral: Dedication to Oskar Panizza; or The Funeral Procession; or To Oskar Panizza]
